mapping.a_element: elastic interface in a random potential with discrete jumps (static avalanches)
mapping.b_element: height functions of random perfect matchings (dimer configurations) on a weighted graph

mapping.a_element: distribution of avalanche sizes and cumulants derived via functional renormalization
mapping.b_element: explicit formulas for surface tension and local Gibbs measure probabilities derived via spectral curve of the Kasteleyn operator

mapping.a_element: epsilon=4-d expansion and tree/1-loop resummation for critical behavior
mapping.b_element: Harnack curve properties of the spectral curve governing phase behavior and fluctuations

mapping.a_element: Burgers equation as a connection to dynamics
mapping.b_element: Ronkin function and its Legendre dual as a connection to surface tension

brief_justification: While both papers study random surfaces and their statistical properties, Paper A focuses on a renormalization-group approach to critical behavior in a disordered elastic system with avalanches, whereas Paper B derives exact probabilistic formulas for dimer models using algebraic geometry (spectral curves and Harnack curves). The underlying machinery—functional renormalization vs. algebraic integrability—differs fundamentally.
